facts.text: Monroe village first called Cheescock’s, renamed Southfields in 1802, and adopted its current name on April 6, 1808, honoring President James Monroe.
The Monroe Historical Society, founded in 1974 and state‑chartered in 1990, preserves the town’s heritage through photographs, artifacts, and written history.
On March 17, 1895, a devastating fire nearly destroyed Monroe’s village center, drawing visitors from across the region to see the ruins.
Monroe once hosted the original headquarters of the Orange and Rockland Electric Company, founded by Roscoe W.
The village sits within Orange County, New York, a region noted for its early colonial history and Native American heritage.
The 1707 Cheesecocks Patent, granted by Queen Anne, encompassed present‑day Monroe and surrounding areas, laying the groundwork for future settlement.
